page: Microsoft Azure B16ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. This processor provides 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz with no hyperthreading. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1822.49, while the multi-core score reaches 28877.71. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1126.93 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 130.2 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core scores are relatively weak, reflecting the single-thread limitations of the Neoverse-N1 architecture. However, multi-threaded operations, such as Geekbench multi-core (9576.0) and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression (72.93 GB/sec), perform in the average tier.

This server type is designed for workloads with burstable CPU requirements, offering a cost-efficient profile for applications that do not continuously utilize full CPU capacity. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for general-purpose tasks, static web serving (benchmarked at 18.84 GB/sec), and database operations (Passmark database score of 11297.67). It is less suited for intensive cryptographic tasks or large-scale LLM inference, as evidenced by weak scores in Passmark CPU encryption and low token generation speeds for 7B models. Appropriate workloads include development environments, code compilation, and intermediate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure B16ps_v2 is an ARM64-based burstable virtual machine within the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor, it features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at 3.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28877.71 and a Passmark CPU score of 11631.88. Single-threaded performance and cryptographic operations are weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1107.0 and a weak Passmark encryption rating. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for burstable, general-purpose workloads, such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, where continuous maximum CPU utilization is not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16ps_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 Ampere Altra vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and average multi-core performance suitable for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16ps_v2 is an ARM64 burstable instance with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, designed for cost-efficient general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine offering balanced memory and multi-core processing for non-continuous, general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for qualitative cost efficiency
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage integration
Delivers average multi-core performance for web serving and database operations
Exhibits lower single-threaded and cryptographic benchmark performance
